docs.title: My documentation
docs.text: My learning process, design workflows, and practical work produced during the Modelling and Fabrication course are all documented on this website.

docs.title: About me
docs.text: My name is Ephron Twagirimana. I am a Software Developer and IoT Specialist based in Rwanda, passionate about using technology to solve community issues. With a background in both technical education and software engineering, I bridge the gap between complex embedded systems and real-world applications.

docs.title: My Journey
docs.text: I got Bachelor's degree from University of Rwanda, College of Science and Technology (School of ICT) in 2023, and be a Network and Internet Technology Teacher in secondary education Transitioned to Software Developer role in late Currently pursuing MSc in IoT Embedded Computing Systems Combining innovative approach with strong technical skills to drive results

docs.title: Assignments
docs.text:
Welcome, My name is Ephron Twagirimana from UR-ACEIoT

Day 1 Activity Day 2 Activity Day 3 Activity Day 4 Activity Day 5 Activity Day 6 Activity Day 7 Activity Day 8 Activity Day 9 Activiy

docs.title: Activity 1: Building a Documentation Website with MkDocs Material
docs.text: Documentation is important in engineering and fabrication projects because it allows users and developers to understand how a system works. In this task, I created a documentation website using MkDocs with the Material for MkDocs theme. MkDocs is a static site generator designed for building project documentation using Markdown files. The Material theme enhances MkDocs by providing a modern interface, navigation features, and responsive design. I Used Python,MkDocs and Material for MkDocs as Tools and Technologies

docs.title: Installation Process I used
docs.text:
• Install Python: python. Verifying "--version/pip --version"

  • Install MkDocs: pip install mkdocs (verify by "mkdocs --version")

  • Create a New MkDocs Project by using "mkdocs new my-project-name" command
  • Installing Material Theme: pip install mkdocs-material

  • Running the Documentation Website: mkdocs serve

  • My Web Pages

docs.title: Activity 2: Publishing Documentation via GitHub Pages
docs.text: After creating a documentation website using MkDocs, the next step was to publish the documentation online so it could be accessed by others through the internet. This was done using GitHub Pages, a service provided by GitHub that allows users to host static websites directly from a repository. I used Git, GitHub and GitHub Pages. I Created a GitHub Repository to my GitHub account, Initialized Git in the Project Folder by using Git commands in the Git Bash terminal: git init, git add ., git add .,git commit -m "", git remote add origin https://github.com/Ephron99/Twagirimana_Ephron_219000099_Modeling-Fabrication.git, git push -u origin main. then Deploy the Site Using MkDocs mkdocs gh-deploy

docs.title: Activity 3: Peer Review
docs.text: In this activity, I reviewed the documentation website created by Mr. Emile, which was developed using MkDocs with the Material for MkDocs and published via GitHub Pages on GitHub. The documentation included well-organized sections for activities and tasks, The pages loaded correctly, and the website was accessible online. This peer review activity helped me understand the importance of clear structure, detailed explanations, and visual support in technical documentation. Reviewing another student’s work also provided ideas that I can use to improve my own documentation website.

docs.title: Digital Modeling for Fabrication
docs.text: Digital modeling allows designers to create precise virtual models before producing physical objects.

docs.title: Activity 1: Creating an L-Shaped Mounting Bracket in 3D Using FreeCAD
docs.text:
In this activity, a 3D model of an L-Shaped Mounting Bracket was created using FreeCAD. An L-shaped mounting bracket is a mechanical component commonly used to support or connect two structures at a 90-degree angle. It usually contains holes for screws or bolts so that it can be fixed to surfaces such as walls, frames, or machines.

I Created a new Body and Sketch.

I Applied the Pad on object.

The final design produced a 3D L-shaped with holes and Fillet.

docs.title: Activity 2: Designing a 2D Vector Press-Fit Box Panel
docs.text:
In this activity, I designed a press-fit box panel using Inkscape.

FreeCad Project

Press-Fit Box Panel

docs.title: PCB Milling Techniques & Fabrication Process
docs.text: On Day 3, we studied Printing Circuit Board (PCB) design as a fundamental step in electronics fabrication.

docs.title: Single-Sided Microcontroller PCB Design using Kicad
docs.text:
In this activity, I designed a single-sided microcontroller board using KiCad.

I used ATtiny45 as board and push button for user input, LED for output indication and ISP programming header for firmware uploading. The push button sends a signal to the microcontroller, which then controls the LED.

I exported Gerber and Drill files that used in Milling & Cutting

Here there is a link for Gerber and Drill files.

Gerber and Drill files

docs.title: PCB in 3D view
docs.text: This is kicad zipped folder:

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
This session focused on understanding the relationship between materials and fabrication methods and how this relationship affects the final design outcome. In digital fabrication, materials are not simply passive elements; they directly influence how a design is created, manufactured, and used.

Different materials have unique properties such as strength, flexibility, thickness, durability, and thermal resistance. These properties determine which fabrication techniques can be applied and how a product performs over time.

docs.title: Objective
docs.text:
• Understand how material properties influence design
  • Explore how different materials behave during fabrication
  • Learn how to choose materials based on fabrication methods
  • Gain hands-on experience through material-based prototyping

In digital fabrication, selecting the correct material is essential because it affects Strength, Flexibility, Durability, and Machinability.

docs.title: Common Materials Used in Digital Fabrication
docs.text:
• Wood

  • Acrylic (Perspex)

  • Plastics

  • Metal (Aluminum / Steel)

During the session, swe explored how different materials behave when subjected to fabrication processes. This hands-on approach helped demonstrate how design choices must adapt to the properties of the selected material.

From this session I gained that Materials play an active role in design and fabrication. materials are not simply passive components but essential factors that influence the entire fabrication process. By understanding how materials interact with fabrication techniques, designers can create more efficient, durable, and functional products.

docs.title: Digital Fabrication I: CNC & Laser Cutting
Introduction
docs.text:
Digital fabrication technologies allow designers to convert digital designs into physical objects using computer-controlled machines. In this session, students were introduced to two important fabrication methods: CNC machining and laser cutting.

These technologies enable precise manufacturing of parts from various materials such as wood, acrylic, plastic, and metal. The session emphasized how digital designs created using CAD or vector software can be directly used to fabricate real-world components.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Laser Cutting uses a focused laser beam to cut or engrave materials with high precision. It is widely used for 2D fabrication and prototyping.

Laser cutting works best with vector designs, which can be created using cutting machine software or others like Inkscape.

docs.title: Preparing Designs for Fabrication
docs.text:
Before sending a design to a fabrication machine, several preparation steps are necessary:

  • File Preparation to ensure correct design dimensions and scale.

  • Machine Settings

Important parameters are Cutting speed, Laser power, Tool diameter and Cutting depth. These parameters depend on the material type and thickness.

Safety

Ensuring proper ventilation during laser cutting and Never leaving machines unattended while operating

From this session, I learned How digital designs are converted into machine instructions, How material properties influence fabrication processes and Basic safety procedures when operating fabrication equipment.

docs.title: Challenge
docs.text:
During the Day 5 session on CNC and laser cutting, a challenge was encountered related to limited access to the Cutting machines.

Although I were able to learn the theoretical concepts and observe the fabrication workflow, not every student had enough time to fabricate their own design during the scheduled session.

This situation occurred because of the number of available machine was only one and every student had to design and cut.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Additive manufacturing, commonly known as 3D printing, is a digital fabrication process where objects are created by depositing material layer by layer according to a digital model. Unlike subtractive manufacturing methods such as milling, additive manufacturing builds the object gradually, which reduces material waste and allows the creation of complex shapes.

In this session, we learned how to fabricate objects using a Ultimaker 3D Printer. The printer produces physical objects from digital designs using thermoplastic filament materials.

The following is Ultimaker 3D Printer

docs.title: Preparing the Model for Printing
docs.text:
A model designed using FreeCAD exported in STL format. The STL file is opened in slicing software such as Ultimaker Cura. Slicing software converts the model into G-code, which the printer uses to control printing movements.

The following images are an object in FreeCAD

After

After printing we Remove the printed object from the build plate and Clean the surface.

From this activity, I gained the ability to understand the principles of additive manufacturing, Preparing models for 3D printing and Operating 3D printer

Printed 3D object

docs.title: Digital Fabrication III: CNC Router Milling & Cutting
Overview
docs.text:
A CNC removes material from a workpiece based on tool paths generated from digital designs. This method is commonly used to manufacture parts from materials.

The machine follows instructions generated from software and moves along multiple axes to cut the material accurately.

Before machining begins I Secure the material on the machine bed, Install the appropriate cutting tool, Set the machine to zero reference point (origin) then Load the machining program. Once the setup is complete, the CNC router begins cutting the material according to the defined toolpaths.

Safety

Working with CNC requires proper safety practices. Keeping hands away from moving tools, and Monitoring the machine during operation.

docs.title: Challenge
docs.text:
During the CNC router milling activity, some I encountered a problem while generating the toolpath for the design. The issue occurred when the software attempted to create the cutting path for the CNC router.

toolpath appeared damaged, meaning that I were missing this activity, the CNC machine could not properly interpret the cutting instructions.

This problem occurred during the preparation stage when converting the design into machining instructions for CNC Machining.
